parameters.query:
SELECT l.id AS lead_id, l.company_name
FROM leads l
JOIN lead_enrichment e ON e.lead_id = l.id
WHERE e.funnel_type = 'info_application_abandon'
  AND l.is_archived = FALSE
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM lead_audits a WHERE a.lead_id = l.id)
ORDER BY l.created_at ASC
LIMIT 20;

INSERT INTO outreach_events (lead_id, event_type, event_data, actor)
VALUES ($1, 'audit', '{"mode":"synthetic_application","reason":"application_funnel_not_auto_auditable"}'::jsonb, 'system');

parameters.content:
## Synth Audit — Application Funnels (Option C)

Application funnels (multi-field / HubSpot "apply today" forms) CANNOT be auto-audited. Instead of stalling those leads (scoring + drafting both require a completed audit), this writes a synthetic 'complete' audit carrying the structural application-abandon gap. They then flow to scoring -> drafting like any lead.

The gap is inferred from the funnel type (how a human strategist assesses an application funnel); we never claim to have opted in.

Flow: enrichment tags funnel_type='info_application_abandon' -> this writes a synth audit -> scoring + drafting pick it up.

Wire: Postgres cred on all 3 DB nodes. Runs every 10 min. Idempotent (skips leads that already have an audit). Uses parameterized queries ($1/$2) so the gap text is safely escaped.

Opt-in funnels still go to the real audit worker. Captcha'd funnels are skipped.
